sql_array: CREATE TABLE instances (id INT PRIMARY KEY, instance_type VARCHAR(50), launch_time TIMESTAMP, state VARCHAR(20), tags VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO instances (id, instance_type, launch_time, state, tags) VALUES (1, 't2.micro', NOW(), 'running', 'environment:production');
INSERT INTO instances (id, instance_type, launch_time, state, tags) VALUES (2, 't2.nano', NOW() - INTERVAL '1 DAY', 'stopped', 'environment:staging');
INSERT INTO instances (id, instance_type, launch_time, state, tags) VALUES (3, 't2.large', NOW(), 'terminated', NULL);
SELECT id, instance_type, launch_time FROM instances WHERE state IS NOT NULL AND launch_time > (SELECT CAST(NOW() AS TIMESTAMP) - INTERVAL '1 DAY' AS TIMESTAMP) ORDER BY launch_time DESC;
SELECT * FROM instances WHERE bif:contains(tags, 'production') AND instance_type IN (SELECT instance_type FROM instances WHERE state = 'running');
SELECT instance_type, COUNT(*) AS instance_count FROM instances GROUP BY instance_type HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 AND MAX(launch_time) <= (SELECT launch_time FROM instances WHERE id = 3);
SELECT id, COALESCE(NULLIF(instance_type, ''), 'unknown') AS resolved_instance_type FROM instances WHERE bif:contains(instance_type, 't2');
SELECT * FROM instances WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM instances WHERE state IS NULL AND bif:contains(state, 'running'));
